summary: Improvement-cycle PR addressing external review #1 (berendt). Three substantive fixes: (1) chainsaw-test.yaml drops `--since=120s` from both the assertion log scrape and the catch-block dump, eliminating the vacuous-pass risk on slow CI clusters where Keystone reaches Ready >120s after pod start (W-001); (2) chainsaw-test.yaml switches `grep -cF` to `grep -F` and prints captured offending log lines in the assertion error (I-003); (3) reconcile_credential_test.go tightens TestCredentialRotationCronJob_CopyKeysInitContainerPreservesNonWorldReadableMode to assert the full `["sh", "-c", ...]` argv shape and a stricter `install -m 0400` substring (I-004). The new `feature:` frontmatter line in keystone-reconciler.md was also reverted (I-001). Two new review_patterns capture the lessons. All controller unit tests pass; go vet clean.

tests_checklist.item: Modified test (TestCredentialRotationCronJob_CopyKeysInitContainerPreservesNonWorldReadableMode) passes against current production code
Tightened argv-shape assertions catch a future swap to `bash` or a non-shell entrypoint
Chainsaw test continues to assert ZERO matches for both fernet and credential paths via independent grep invocations
Chainsaw failure now echoes the offending log line directly (not only in catch-block dump)

code_quality_checklist.item: Diff is minimal and scoped to the external review feedback — no unrelated edits
Bash logic in chainsaw-test.yaml correctly handles 0/1/N matches under `set -euo pipefail` (grep || true, [ -n ] guard, wc -l for count)
Stale `strings.Contains` is no longer referenced in the modified test, but the `strings` import remains in use elsewhere in the file (lines 178, 902-907) — no unused-import lint issue
Existing patterns followed: gomega NewGomegaWithT, CC-0099 tagging in messages, find-by-name container lookup, REQ-008 regression guards retained
Catch-block heading updated consistently with the actual log retrieval (`(last 2000 lines)`)

security_checklist.item: No new attack surface — change is test-and-doc-only
Removing `--since=120s` does not weaken the security assertion; it strengthens it by widening the observation window to all 2000 retained lines

architecture_checklist.item: Solution applied at the narrowest scope — only changes in scope are in the review-scope-restriction list
Symmetry between fernet and credential test argv-shape assertions now matches (both lock Command[0]/[1]/[2])

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic introduced; the two grep blocks remain independent by design (REQ-006 demands separate failures)
FERNET_COUNT/CRED_COUNT computed only inside the failure branch, not eagerly

fail_fast_checklist.item: set -euo pipefail retained; FAIL=1 + final `exit $FAIL` preserve aggregate-then-exit semantics

defensive_checklist.item: External boundary (kubectl logs output) handled: `2>&1` capture, `|| true` after grep to tolerate zero-match

issues_found.description: The metadata files describe the `feature:` frontmatter outcome incorrectly. (a) `.planwerk/reviews/CC-0099-github-review-by-berendt-external-1.json` `resolution_summary` states: "the `feature:` field was preserved as a positive deviation". (b) `.planwerk/review_patterns/flag-duplicated-logic-blocks-with-sync-cross-references.md` CC-0099 entry says: "Reviewer flagged for ASK; author intentionally preserved the field as a positive deviation." In reality, the diff at `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md:1-7` REMOVES the `feature:` line entirely (commit 9b16ddd0 message confirms: "drop the new `feature:` frontmatter line (I-001)"). The two meta files therefore contradict the actual implementation and the commit message — they will mislead future reviewers consulting either record.
issues_found.fix: Update both records to reflect that the `feature:` frontmatter was REMOVED (I-001 was applied, not deferred). In the review JSON, replace the clause about the `feature:` field with: 'the `feature:` frontmatter was removed per I-001'. In the review_patterns file, replace the CC-0099 Fix line with: 'Removed the `feature:` frontmatter line entirely — no other reference doc carries the field and no schema consumes it.'
issues_found.evidence:
Diff for docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md:
  -feature: CC-0013, CC-0015, CC-0038, CC-0057, CC-0058, CC-0064, CC-0065, CC-0067, CC-0068, CC-0071, CC-0072, CC-0073, CC-0074, CC-0077, CC-0078, CC-0079, CC-0080, CC-0081, CC-0087, CC-0095, CC-0099
Commit 9b16ddd0 message: 'docs/reference/keystone/keystone-reconciler.md: drop the new `feature:` frontmatter line (I-001)'.
Review JSON resolution_summary: 'the `feature:` field was preserved as a positive deviation'.
review_patterns md CC-0099 Fix: 'Reviewer flagged for ASK; author intentionally preserved the field as a positive deviation.'

suggested_improvements: Optional follow-up: extend `TestIntegration_CronJobDetailedSpec` with a credential-rotation parallel that uses full-slice equality on the init-container Command, mirroring the existing fernet integration assertion (I-002, intentionally deferred per reviewer guidance).

Optional follow-up: update PR description to state effective projected mode is `0o440` (not `0o450`), matching kubelet's actual `mode | (mode & 0700) >> 3` projection (I-003 from external review, doc-only).
next_steps: Fix the two metadata inconsistencies described in DA3 so the audit trail accurately records that the `feature:` frontmatter was removed.
